sections.paragraph:
πρεσβύτερος, α, ον [πρέσβυς ‘old man, elder’]

—1. ‘ranked as superior in age’, older

—a. of advantage in age over another, freq. w. nominal aspect the older one Lk 15:25; J 8:9; Ac 2:17; 1 Ti 5:1f.

—b. by ext., through association of ancestral respect w. remembrance of a venerable past, as pl. noun elders, ancestors Mt 15:2; Mk 7:3, 5; Hb 11:2.

—2.‘ranked in terms of official responsibility’, elders

—a. Judean officials: in councils outside Jerusalem Lk 7:3; in Jerusalem, the Sanhedrin Mt 16:21; 21:23; 26:47, 57; 27:20; Mk 8:31; Lk 9:22; 20:1; 22:52; Ac 4:5, 23; 6:12; 25:15.

—b. leaders in a congregational setting of Christ followers Ac 11:30; 14:23; 15:2; 1 Ti 5:17, 19; Tit 1:5; Js 5:14; 1 Pt 5:1, 5.

—c. in ext. sense, the twenty-four heavenly council members Rv 4:4, 10 and freq. in Rv.

—d. one serving in some now unknown capacity to which the first auditors were privy, ὁ πρεσβύτερος the elder 2 J 1; 3 J 1.
